# UC-17.1.3 · VLAN Assignment Audit

## Description

Dynamic VLAN assignments reflect authorization decisions. Anomalous placements may indicate policy misconfiguration or attacks.

## Value

Dynamic VLAN assignments reflect authorization decisions. Anomalous placements may indicate policy misconfiguration or attacks.

## Implementation

Track VLAN assignments per endpoint. Maintain expected VLAN lookup by user role/device type. Alert on unexpected VLAN placements. Audit authorization policy effectiveness.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_cisco-ise`, HPE Aruba ClearPass syslog, Forescout CounterACT syslog.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: ISE authorization logs (VLAN assignment).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Track VLAN assignments per endpoint. Maintain expected VLAN lookup by user role/device type. Alert on unexpected VLAN placements. Audit authorization policy effectiveness.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:auth"
| where assigned_vlan!=expected_vlan
| table _time, endpoint_mac, username, assigned_vlan, expected_vlan, authorization_policy
```

Understanding this SPL

**VLAN Assignment Audit** — Dynamic VLAN assignments reflect authorization decisions. Anomalous placements may indicate policy misconfiguration or attacks.

Documented **Data sources**: ISE authorization logs (VLAN assignment). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_cisco-ise`, HPE Aruba ClearPass syslog, Forescout CounterACT syslog. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: nac; **sourcetype**: cisco:ise:auth. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=nac, sourcetype="cisco:ise:auth". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where assigned_vlan!=expected_vlan` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **VLAN Assignment Audit**): table _time, endpoint_mac, username, assigned_vlan, expected_vlan, authorization_policy

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Cisco ISE Live Log and endpoint details for the same sessions, MACs, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (VLAN assignments), Pie chart (assignments by VLAN), Bar chart (unexpected placements).

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:auth"
| where assigned_vlan!=expected_vlan
| table _time, endpoint_mac, username, assigned_vlan, expected_vlan, authorization_policy
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (VLAN assignments), Pie chart (assignments by VLAN), Bar chart (unexpected placements).
